spec.rationale: The migrate-emptydir-to-pvc-gitops-v1 workflow is the sole available action type and is a strong structural match for the primary root cause: postgres-emptydir is a stateful PostgreSQL database running on a 4Gi emptyDir volume, which is exactly the use case this workflow targets. The workflow performs a live pg_dump backup, commits an emptyDir-to-PVC migration via GitOps (ArgoCD), and restores data to a persistent volume — eliminating the structural cause of the node-local disk growth. This is a proactive signal (pod is still running, backup is feasible per workflow guidance). Confidence is modestly capped at 0.82 rather than higher because: (1) other workloads (log-collector, nginx-cache, redis-scratch) also contribute disk pressure and are NOT addressed by this workflow; (2) ArgoCD management of the namespace is inferred but not explicitly confirmed in the enrichment context; (3) the workflow targets one of four disk-pressure sources — additional manual remediation of the other three deployments may be required.
